<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T062935-e4ef6b7f/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T062935-e4ef6b7f/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T062935-e4ef6b7f/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T062935-e4ef6b7f/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T062935-e4ef6b7f/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-d8474e0e">
    <title>FULLY retire 'ddx agent' CLI surface — no deprecation period; replace with ddx run/try/work</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
All 21 ddx agent subcommands remain live in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85 — none are Hidden, none are Deprecated. The migration to ddx run/try/work was supposed to happen but only ddx work was added. Operators still use ddx agent surface, and skill manifests + docs still reference ddx agent.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85: 21 cobra command registrations for ddx agent subcommands (run, execute-bead, execute-loop, benchmark, condense, capabilities, replay, list, doctor, log, etc.) are all live. None set Hidden=true or Deprecated.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: execute-loop and execute-bead are aliases; after C13 (ddx-387a0178), ddx work and ddx try replace these.
- .agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, skills/: skill manifests reference 'ddx agent run' and 'ddx agent execute-bead'.
- docs/: README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website/content/docs/cli/_index.md reference ddx agent.
- Depends on C13 (ddx-387a0178) which adds ddx try CLI — cannot retire ddx agent until ddx try exists.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Delete cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go and all cli/cmd/agent_*.go sibling files.
2. Verify: ddx run + ddx try + ddx work cover every prior ddx agent operation:
   - ddx agent run → ddx run
   - ddx agent execute-loop → ddx work
   - ddx agent execute-bead → ddx try
   - ddx agent list → ddx agent list removed (or migrated to ddx harness list)
   - ddx agent doctor → ddx doctor
   - ddx agent log → ddx log (or removed)
3. Update skill manifests to use new commands.
4. Update docs (README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website CLI docs).
5. Update lefthook hooks if any reference ddx agent.

NON-SCOPE
- Adding new flags or subcommands to ddx run/try/work (out of scope).
- Deprecation period (user directive: 'completely gone').
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go and all cli/cmd/agent_*.go files deleted.
2. grep -rn 'ddx agent' cli/ docs/ .agents/skills/ .claude/skills/ skills/ returns 0 hits in non-deleted files.
3. ddx run, ddx try, ddx work cover all prior ddx agent operations (verified by checklist in commit body).
4. Skill manifests updated to new commands.
5. README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website CLI docs updated.
6. Lefthook hooks updated if they referenced ddx agent.
7. cd cli && go build ./... green.
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
10. For each ddx agent subcommand removed, name the test in cli/cmd/{run,try,work}_test.go that asserts the new entry point preserves the prior behavior. At minimum: TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn, TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding, TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead, TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop, TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2 (matches ddx-c8f79963 / try.Attempt disposition contract).
11. Test surface classification — cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead*_test.go (~3,491 lines: agent_execute_bead_test.go, agent_execute_bead_e2e_test.go, agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go, execute_bead_e2e_routing_test.go) and cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop*_test.go: produce .ddx/executions/<run-id>/agent-cmd-test-migration.md classifying each test as (a) cobra-flag-parsing-only → delete, (b) engine-via-harness → migrate to call ExecuteBeadWithConfig or try.Attempt directly, (c) unique behavior → re-target to ddx try / ddx work cobra surface with same assertions. Each row of the audit names the test function and the disposition.
12. DEPENDENCY ddx-424fdac4 provides the governing-doc authorization for removing the ddx agent CLI surface (FEAT-001 mount removal, ADR-024 routing-boundary ratification per ddx-3ee4ddcf NON-SCOPE clause). Verify ddx-424fdac4 is closed before this bead's removal step.
13. DEPENDENCY ddx-ddacd4ff (C14) AC #7-9 lift agent.RecoverOrphans, agent.BuildLandingGateContext, and agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService from the cobra wrapper (cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:160-217) into the engine or try.Attempt before deletion. This bead's full retirement step inherits those lifts as prerequisites.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
Re-sequenced under ddx-f088a3fd after operator decision: remove, do not deprecate. Previous deps on alias-preserving ddx-387a0178/ddx-3ee4ddcf were removed because those beads conflict with the no-alias plan. This bead now follows the spec ratification bead ddx-424fdac4.
    </notes>
